verses.text: Woe to the shepherds who feed themselves, eating the fat and wearing the wool, but not feeding the flock. Self-serving leaders. Those set over God's people too often serve only themselves.
Woe to the shepherds who feed themselves, eating the fat and wearing the wool, but not feeding the flock. Self-serving leaders. Those set over God's people too often serve only themselves.
Woe to the shepherds who feed themselves, eating the fat and wearing the wool, but not feeding the flock. Self-serving leaders. Those set over God's people too often serve only themselves.
They have not healed the sick nor sought the lost; the sheep wandered, and 'none did search.' Neglect. Faithless shepherds abandon the very flock entrusted to them.
They have not healed the sick nor sought the lost; the sheep wandered, and 'none did search.' Neglect. Faithless shepherds abandon the very flock entrusted to them.
They have not healed the sick nor sought the lost; the sheep wandered, and 'none did search.' Neglect. Faithless shepherds abandon the very flock entrusted to them.
God is against the shepherds; He will require His flock at their hand and deliver His sheep from their mouth. Accountability. The Lord will rescue His people from those who prey on them.
God is against the shepherds; He will require His flock at their hand and deliver His sheep from their mouth. Accountability. The Lord will rescue His people from those who prey on them.
God is against the shepherds; He will require His flock at their hand and deliver His sheep from their mouth. Accountability. The Lord will rescue His people from those who prey on them.
God is against the shepherds; He will require His flock at their hand and deliver His sheep from their mouth. Accountability. The Lord will rescue His people from those who prey on them.
'I, even I, will both search my sheep, and seek them out'; as a shepherd seeks his flock, so will He. God Himself shepherds. Where men fail, the Lord undertakes His people's care.
'I, even I, will both search my sheep, and seek them out'; as a shepherd seeks his flock, so will He. God Himself shepherds. Where men fail, the Lord undertakes His people's care.
He will gather them from the nations and feed them in a good fold on the mountains of Israel. Brought home. The Shepherd leads His scattered flock to rich pasture.
He will gather them from the nations and feed them in a good fold on the mountains of Israel. Brought home. The Shepherd leads His scattered flock to rich pasture.
'I will feed my flock, and I will cause them to lie down'; He seeks the lost and binds up the broken. Tender care. The Good Shepherd's heart is toward the weak and wandering.
'I will feed my flock, and I will cause them to lie down'; He seeks the lost and binds up the broken. Tender care. The Good Shepherd's heart is toward the weak and wandering.
He will judge between the sheep; is it small that the strong eat the good pasture and trample the rest? Judgment within. God marks how the strong treat the weak among His people.
He will judge between the sheep; is it small that the strong eat the good pasture and trample the rest? Judgment within. God marks how the strong treat the weak among His people.
He will judge between the sheep; is it small that the strong eat the good pasture and trample the rest? Judgment within. God marks how the strong treat the weak among His people.
He will judge between fat and lean; because the strong thrust the weak, He will save His flock. The bullies answered. God defends the helpless from the harsh among them.
He will judge between fat and lean; because the strong thrust the weak, He will save His flock. The bullies answered. God defends the helpless from the harsh among them.
He will judge between fat and lean; because the strong thrust the weak, He will save His flock. The bullies answered. God defends the helpless from the harsh among them.
'I will set up one shepherd over them... my servant David,' and the LORD will be their God. The Messiah promised. Christ, the Son of David, is the one true Shepherd-King.
'I will set up one shepherd over them... my servant David,' and the LORD will be their God. The Messiah promised. Christ, the Son of David, is the one true Shepherd-King.
He will make 'a covenant of peace,' the land shall yield her fruit, and they shall dwell safely. Restored blessing. Under the Shepherd's reign His people are secure and provided for.
He will make 'a covenant of peace,' the land shall yield her fruit, and they shall dwell safely. Restored blessing. Under the Shepherd's reign His people are secure and provided for.
He will make 'a covenant of peace,' the land shall yield her fruit, and they shall dwell safely. Restored blessing. Under the Shepherd's reign His people are secure and provided for.
They shall be a prey no more; He will raise 'a plant of renown'; 'ye my flock... are men, and I am your God.' Sweet assurance. The covenant ends in the nearness of God to His people.
They shall be a prey no more; He will raise 'a plant of renown'; 'ye my flock... are men, and I am your God.' Sweet assurance. The covenant ends in the nearness of God to His people.
They shall be a prey no more; He will raise 'a plant of renown'; 'ye my flock... are men, and I am your God.' Sweet assurance. The covenant ends in the nearness of God to His people.
